Off Calendar Mar-13-2024 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Timely Amend
Off Calendar Mar-11-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT's Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Timely Amend.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's unopposed "motion [to] dismiss for failure to timely amend" is granted.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-04-2024 Continued To Mar-27-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-15-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

RE: DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT's DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person.

The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: San Francisco Unified School District's demurrer to Plaintiffs Maria De Arenaza and Daniel Vinas' complaint, for which no opposition was received, is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. The complaint fails to state facts sufficient to state a cause of action upon which relief can be granted. (See Code Civ. Proc., Sec. 430.10, subd. (e).) Indeed, the complaint includes no discernible facts and the court is unable to discern cognizable facts from the attachments, many of which are in Spanish unaccompanied by any translation. Additionally, the complaint is uncertain in that it is ambiguous and unintelligible as submitted. (See Code Civ. Proc., Sec. 430.10, subd. (f).) Further, the complaint is uncertain in that it fails to identify a statutory basis for SFUSD's alleged liability. (See Code Civ. Proc., Sec. 430.10, subd. (f); C.A. v. William S. Hart Union High Sch. Dist. (2012) 53 Cal.4th 861.) Finally, Plaintiff Vinas is a minor without the legal capacity to sue. (See Code Civ. Proc., Sec. 430.10, subd. (b).) It appears that Vinas will have to secure a guardian ad litem to pursue his claims on his behalf and the guardian ad litem, if not a licensed attorney, may need to secure the services of a license attorney. Any amended complaint must be filed within 20 days of service of notice of entry of this order. Please note that by law, California courts must conduct proceedings in English, meaning any amended complaint must be in English and any attachments written in a language other than English must be accompanied by a written translation, certified under oath by a qualified interpreter. (See Cal. Rules of Ct., rule 3.1110(g).)

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

***After hearing, an order is signed.
